title: KeyX Pro verified product facts v1
content: Verified facts currently available for KeyX Pro: product name is KeyX Pro; confirmed features are gasket mount structure, hot-swappable switches, RGB backlighting and wireless connectivity. Do not claim battery capacity, latency, Bluetooth version, key count, weight, material, price, warranty, certifications, awards, shipping, discounts, user counts, review scores or performance test results unless separately provided by a verified product source.

title: Gasket mount benefit mapping
content: Use gasket mount as a typing-feel benefit, not a medical or ergonomic claim. Safe framing: softer typing feel, cushioned key press, more stable sound and feel, a calmer typing experience. Avoid: reduces wrist strain, prevents pain, cures fatigue, clinically ergonomic, medically beneficial.

title: Hot-swappable switches benefit mapping
content: Use hot-swappable switches as a personalization and maintenance benefit. Safe framing: users can adjust typing feel, test different switch styles and refresh the board without positioning the product as a repair guarantee. Avoid unsupported claims about switch lifespan, compatibility with every switch or tool-free repair unless verified.

title: RGB backlighting benefit mapping
content: Use RGB backlighting as an identity, visibility and desk-style benefit. Safe framing: customize the desk mood, make the setup more recognizable, support low-light desk use. Avoid unsupported claims about productivity gains, eye protection or professional calibration.

title: Wireless connectivity benefit mapping
content: Use wireless connectivity as a desk-clutter and workflow flexibility benefit. Safe framing: cleaner desk, fewer cables, easier switching between work modes. Avoid: zero lag, minimal lag, low-latency, gaming-grade latency, specific Bluetooth version, battery life or connection range unless verified by product testing.

title: Global keyboard brand voice guide
content: Brand voice should be practical, confident and user-centered. Prefer clear benefits over hype. Use concrete work, creator and desk setup scenarios. Avoid attacking competitors, exaggerated superlatives, fake testimonials, fake awards and unsupported numerical claims. Approved tone words: professional, energetic, premium, friendly, technical but accessible.

title: EDM baseline compliance: CAN-SPAM risk checklist
content: For US-facing commercial EDM, use accurate sender and subject information, avoid deceptive claims, include a clear opt-out or preference management path and include a valid physical postal address. Treat this as a marketing risk checklist, not legal advice. If sender address, opt-out link or target market rule is missing, ask for human confirmation before publication.
source_name: FTC CAN-SPAM Act compliance guide for business

title: Advertising claim substantiation rule
content: Objective marketing claims should be truthful, not misleading and supported before publication. Do not publish claims such as zero lag, doubles productivity, reduces pain, medical grade, FDA approved, award-winning or best unless there is documentary evidence in the product facts or knowledge base. If evidence is missing, remove the claim or ask the team to provide a verified source.
source_name: FTC advertising and marketing basics; ASA CAP Code Section 3

title: Competitor and trademark safety rule
content: Avoid directly naming competitor keyboard brands in generated marketing copy unless the campaign has legal approval and a documented comparison basis. Safer alternatives: other compact keyboards, many enthusiast boards, traditional office keyboards, older wired setups. Do not claim the product is better than a named competitor without evidence.

title: Blog structure: remote work productivity angle
content: Recommended Blog structure for remote work: title, desk/workday pain point, product feature to user benefit mapping, practical scenario, proof boundary, CTA. Do not add EDM unsubscribe footer. Do not invent research data or productivity percentages. Strong CTA examples: explore the setup, learn how the keyboard fits your workday, compare features on the product page.

title: EDM structure: product launch email
content: Recommended EDM structure: subject line, preview text, short opener, three benefit bullets, CTA, unsubscribe or preference-management footer. Keep claims conservative and brief. Do not include fake limited-time discount, fake free shipping, fake warranty or fake user review. If target market is unknown, keep compliance footer generic and ask human reviewer to confirm final sender and address requirements.

title: Approved Blog Example · Remote Work Desk Upgrade
content: Title: A Cleaner Keyboard Setup for Long Workdays. Open with the everyday reality of remote work: switching between writing, meetings and planning while managing desk clutter. Introduce KeyX Pro through verified features only: gasket mount structure for a softer typing feel, hot-swappable switches for personalizing the board, RGB backlighting for a more recognizable desk and wireless connectivity for fewer cables. Explain benefits through practical scenarios rather than numerical claims. CTA: Explore how KeyX Pro can fit into your daily desk setup.

title: Approved Blog Example · Creator Desk Personalization
content: Title: Build a Keyboard Setup That Feels Like Your Workspace. Start from creator workflows: editing, writing, streaming notes and visual desk identity. Position KeyX Pro as a flexible keyboard for users who care about feel and setup expression. Use hot-swappable switches as the customization angle, RGB as visual identity and wireless connectivity as desk flexibility. Avoid claiming higher creative output or measurable productivity gains. CTA: Discover the KeyX Pro setup options.

title: Approved Blog Example · Enthusiast Typing Feel
content: Title: Why Typing Feel Matters in a Daily Keyboard. Explain enthusiast concepts in accessible language: gasket mount can create a softer and more cushioned feel; hot-swappable switches let users experiment with different switch preferences; RGB and wireless features support desk personalization and flexibility. Keep the explanation educational and avoid attacking competitors. CTA: Learn more about how KeyX Pro balances feel, flexibility and desk style.

title: Approved EDM Example · KeyX Pro Launch
content: Subject: Meet KeyX Pro, built for a cleaner desk. Preview: A softer typing feel, flexible switches and fewer cables. Body: Meet KeyX Pro, a keyboard for workdays that move between focus, calls and creative tasks. Benefit bullets: gasket mount structure for a cushioned typing feel; hot-swappable switches for a more personal setup; RGB backlighting and wireless connectivity for a desk that feels flexible and easy to recognize. CTA: Explore KeyX Pro. Footer: You can unsubscribe or manage preferences at any time.

title: Approved EDM Example · Educational Nurture
content: Subject: What makes a keyboard feel better every day? Preview: A quick guide to feel, flexibility and desk setup. Body: A better keyboard story should not start with hype. It starts with how the board feels and how it fits the desk. KeyX Pro combines a gasket mount structure, hot-swappable switches, RGB backlighting and wireless connectivity so users can tune the experience around their work style. CTA: See the KeyX Pro feature guide. Footer: You can unsubscribe or manage preferences at any time.

title: Approved EDM Example · Safe Rewrite for Risky Claims
content: Use this pattern when a brief asks for risky claims such as 100% no lag, doubles productivity or health benefits. Safe rewrite: Focus on verified features and subjective experience. Example: Instead of saying zero lag or guaranteed productivity, say KeyX Pro helps create a cleaner, more flexible desk setup with wireless connectivity, a cushioned typing feel and switch customization. CTA: Explore the features. Footer: You can unsubscribe or manage preferences at any time.

title: Approved Blog Example · Safe Rewrite for Unsupported Claims
content: Use this pattern when a Blog brief asks for unsupported claims. Structure: acknowledge the user need, remove unsupported numbers or certifications, explain verified features, add a proof boundary and end with a conservative CTA. Example: KeyX Pro should not be described as FDA approved, award-winning, zero-lag or proven to reduce pain unless verified evidence is provided. Instead, describe gasket mount feel, hot-swappable customization, RGB desk identity and wireless desk flexibility.
